question_text: What is the main difference between the particle arrangement in a liquid and a gas?

options.label: A
options.text: Liquids have larger particles than gases

options.label: B
options.text: Gas particles are much further apart and move faster than liquid particles

options.label: C
options.text: Liquid particles don't move at all while gas particles move

options.label: D
options.text: There is no difference - liquids and gases are the same

correct_answer: Gas particles are much further apart and move faster than liquid particles
explanation: In the particle model, gases have particles that are very far apart with large spaces between them, and these particles move rapidly and randomly in all directions. Liquids have particles that are much closer together and move more slowly around each other. This difference in spacing and movement explains why gases can spread out to fill any container, while liquids maintain a fixed volume.
